sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the blocks are stacked vertically: red on top, blue in middle, green on bottom. The path has been retracted. The player is on the left side. I need to understand the collection mechanic better.
Looking at the image, the three blocks (red, blue, green) are now stacked vertically next to the player. The path was retracted. Let me check the positions and try to extend RIGHT to collect the red block first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] >= 18 and min_r < 50:
        print(f"Player: rows={min_r}-{min_r+5}")
    if 10 <= min_r <= 45 and n['color'] in ('R', 'b', 'N'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows={min_r}-{min_r+3}")

# Check bottom panel
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(56, 62):
    print(f"Row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][20:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the blocks are stacked vertically:
- R: rows 25-28 (above player)
- Player: rows 30-35
- b: rows 31-34 (aligned with player!)
- N: rows 37-40 (below player)
The blue block is aligned with the player! The bottom panel shows all blocks as uncollected (RRRR, NNNN, bbbb). Let me try extending RIGHT to collect the blue block.
Wait, but earlier when I extended RIGHT, the path touched the blue block but it wasn't collected. Let me try again now that the blocks are in a different configuration.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blue block at 31-34, player at 30-35 - aligned!
# Extend RIGHT to collect blue
action(['RIGHT'] * 10)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
